title: festuca pumila subsp. scoparia
intro: Festuca pumila subsp. scoparia, also known as Alpine Fescue, is a small, perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Alpine Fescue include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Alpine Fescue can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
